sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: αἰαῖ ·
sections.translation: Alas ! You foreign women , where can I escape , flying through the clear sky or over the sea , which bull-headed Ocean rolls about as he circles the world in his embrace ?

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν , Ἑλένης πρόσπολʼ . Ἰδαῖον κάρα ;
sections.translation: What is it , Helen ’ s slave , creature from Ida ?

sections.label: Section 1381
sections.original: Ἴλιον Ἴλιον , ὤμοι μοι ,
sections.translation: Ilium , Ilium , oh me ! city of Phrygia , and Ida ’ s holy hill with fruitful soil , how I mourn for your destruction a shrill song

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.original: βαρβάρῳ βοᾷ διʼ ὀρνιθόγονον
sections.translation: with barbarian cry ; destroyed through her beauty , born from a bird , swan-feathered , Leda ’ s cub , hellish Helen ! to be a curse to Apollo ’ s tower of polished stone . Ah ! Alas !

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.original: ἰαλέμων ἰαλέμων
sections.translation: woe to Dardania , its wailing , wailing , for the horsemanship of Ganymede , bedfellow of Zeus .

sections.label: Section 1393
sections.original: σαφῶς λέγʼ ἡμῖν αὔθʼ ἕκαστα τἀν δόμοις .
sections.translation: Tell us clearly each event within the house . for till now I have been guessing at what I do not clearly understand .

sections.label: Section 1395
sections.original: αἴλινον αἴλινον ἀρχὰν θανάτου
sections.translation: Ah , for Linus ! Ah , for Linus ! That is what barbarians say , alas , in their eastern tongue as a prelude to death , whenever royal blood is spilled upon the ground by deadly iron blades .

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.original: ἦλθον ἐς δόμους , ἵνʼ αὔθʼ ἕ-
sections.translation: To tell you everything in turn , they came into the house , two twin lions of Hellas ; one was called the general ’ s son ; the other was the son of Strophius , a crafty plotter , like Odysseus , treacherous in silence ,

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.original: πιστὸς δὲ φίλοις , θρασὺς εἰς ἀλκάν ,
sections.translation: but true to his friends , bold for the fight , clever in war and a deadly serpent . Curse him for his quiet plotting , the villain !

sections.label: Section 1409
sections.original: οἳ δὲ πρὸς θρόνους ἔσω
sections.translation: In they came to the throne of the wife of Paris the archer ,

sections.label: Section 1410
sections.original: γυναικός , ὄμμα δακρύοις
sections.translation: faces wet with tears , and took their seats in all humility , one on this side , one on that , each with weapons . They threw , they threw their suppliant arms round the knees

sections.label: Section 1415
sections.original: Ἑλένας ἄμφω .
sections.translation: of Helen . Her Phrygian servants sprang up frantic , frantic ; they called to each other in terror that there was treachery .

sections.label: Section 1420
sections.original: κἀδόκει τοῖς μὲν οὔ ,
sections.translation: To some there seemed no cause , but others thought that the viper who killed his mother was entangling the daughter of Tyndareus in the snare of his plot .

sections.label: Section 1425
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἦσθα ποῦ τότʼ ; ἢ πάλαι φεύγεις φόβῳ ,
sections.translation: And where were you ? fled long before in terror ?

sections.label: Section 1426
sections.original: Φρυγίοις ἔτυχον Φρυγίοισι νόμοις
sections.translation: It happened that I , in Phrygian style , Phrygian , was wafting the breeze , the breeze by the curls of Helen , Helen , with a round feathered fan , before her face ,

sections.label: Section 1430
sections.original: ἀίσσων βαρβάροις νόμοισιν .
sections.translation: in barbarian style ; and she was twisting flax on her distaff with her fingers , and letting her yarn fall on the floor , for she wanted to sew with her flax purple cloth

sections.label: Section 1435
sections.original: ματα συστολίσαι χρῄζουσα λίνῳ ,
sections.translation: as adornment for the tomb from the Trojan spoils , a gift to Clytemnestra .

sections.label: Section 1437
sections.original: προσεῖπεν δʼ Ὀρέστας
sections.translation: Orestes said to the Spartan girl : Daughter of Zeus , get up from your chair

sections.label: Section 1440
sections.original: πέδῳ δεῦρʼ ἀποστᾶσα κλισμοῦ ,
sections.translation: and come here to the old hearth of Pelops , our ancestor , to hear something I have to say . He led her , led her , and she followed ,

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.original: οὐ πρόμαντις ὧν ἔμελλεν ·
sections.translation: no prophet of the future . But his accomplice , the Phocian villain , was off on other business : Out of my way ! Well , Phrygians always were cowards . So he shut them up in different parts of the house , some in the stables , others in the halls ,

sections.label: Section 1450
sections.original: έδραισι , τοὺς δʼ ἐκεῖσʼ ἐκεῖθεν ἄλλον ἄλ-
sections.translation: one here , one there , disposing of them severally at a distance from their mistress .

sections.label: Section 1452
sections.original: τί τοὐπὶ τῷδε συμφορᾶς ἐγίγνετο ;
sections.translation: What happened next ?

sections.label: Section 1453
sections.original: Ἰδαία μᾶτερ
sections.translation: Mother of Ida , great , great mother !

sections.label: Section 1455
sections.original: αἰαὶ φονίων παθέων ἀνόμων
sections.translation: Oh ! the murderous scenes and lawless wickedness that I saw , I saw , in the palace ! They drew forth swords from hiding under their purple-bordered cloaks , each darting his eye a different way , lest anyone should be near . Like boar of the hills ,

sections.label: Section 1460
sections.original: ναικὸς ἀντίοι σταθέντες
sections.translation: they stood opposite the woman and said : You will die , you will die ; your cowardly husband is killing you , because he betrayed his brother ’ s son to death in Argos .

sections.label: Section 1465
sections.original: ἃ δʼ ἀνίαχεν ἴ-
sections.translation: She screamed , oh , oh ! she screamed , and brought down her white arm upon her breast and beat her poor head ; then turned her golden-sandalled steps in flight , in flight ; but Orestes got before her in his Mycenean boots and clutched his fingers in her hair ,

sections.label: Section 1470
sections.original: Μυκηνίδʼ ἀρβύλαν προβάς ,
sections.translation: and , bending back her neck on to her left shoulder , was on the point of driving the black sword into her throat .

sections.label: Section 1473
sections.original: ποῦ δʼ ἦτʼ ἀμύνειν οἱ κατὰ στέγας Φρύγες ;
sections.translation: Where were you Phrygians in the house to help her ?

sections.label: Section 1474
sections.original: ἰαχᾷ
sections.translation: With a loud cry from the house we battered down with bars the doors and doorposts where we had been ,

sections.label: Section 1475
sections.original: βοηδρομοῦμεν ἄλλος ἄλλοθεν στέγης ,
sections.translation: and ran to her assistance from every direction , one with stones , another with javelins , a third with a drawn sword ; but Pylades came to meet us , undaunted , like

sections.label: Section 1480
sections.original: τωρ ὁ Φρύγιος ἢ τρικόρυθος Αἴας ,
sections.translation: Hector of Troy or Ajax triple-plumed , as I saw him , saw him , in Priam ’ s gateway ; and we met at sword ’ s point . But then it was very clear how the Phrygians were ,

sections.label: Section 1485
sections.original: ἥσσονες Ἑλλάδος ἐγενόμεθʼ αἰχμᾶς ,
sections.translation: how much less we were in battle strength to the Hellene might . There was one man gone in flight , another slain , another wounded , yet another pleading to stave off death ; but we escaped under cover of the darkness ; while some were falling , some were about to fall , and others were lying dead .

sections.label: Section 1490
sections.original: ἔμολε δʼ ἁ τάλαινʼ Ἑρμιόνα δόμους
sections.translation: And just as her unhappy mother sank to the ground to die , the luckless Hermione came in . Those two , like Bacchantes when they drop the thyrsus for a mountain cub , rushed and seized her ; then turned again to the daughter of Zeus to slay her ; but she had vanished from the room ,

sections.label: Section 1495
sections.original: ἐγένετο διαπρὸ δωμάτων
sections.translation: passing right through the house , O Zeus and Earth and light and night ! whether by magic spells or wizards ’ arts or heavenly theft .

sections.label: Section 1499
sections.original: τὰ δʼ ὕστερʼ οὐκέτʼ οἶδα · δρα-
sections.translation: What happened afterwards I do not know ; for I stole out of the palace , a runaway .

sections.label: Section 1500
sections.original: πολύπονα δὲ πολύπονα πάθεα
sections.translation: So Menelaus endured his painful , painful suffering to recover his wife Helen from Troy to no purpose .

sections.label: Section 1503
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἀμείβει καινὸν ἐκ καινῶν τόδε ·
sections.translation: And look , here is a strange sight succeeding others ; for I see Orestes sword in hand before the palace ,

sections.label: Section 1505
sections.original: βαίνοντʼ Ὀρέστην ἐπτοημένῳ ποδί .
sections.translation: advancing with excited steps .

sections.label: Section 1506
sections.original: ποῦ ʼστιν οὗτος ὃς πέφευγεν ἐκ δόμων τοὐμὸν ξίφος ;
sections.translation: Where is the one who fled from the palace to escape my sword ?

sections.label: Section 1507
sections.original: προσκυνῶ σʼ , ἄναξ , νόμοισι βαρβάροισι προσπίτνων .
sections.translation: Before you I prostrate myself , lord , and supplicate you in my foreign way .

sections.label: Section 1508
sections.original: οὐκ ἐν Ἰλίῳ τάδʼ ἐστίν , ἀλλʼ ἐν Ἀργείᾳ χθονί .
sections.translation: We are not in Ilium , but the land of Argos .

sections.label: Section 1509
sections.original: πανταχοῦ ζῆν ἡδὺ μᾶλλον ἢ θανεῖν τοῖς σώφροσιν .
sections.translation: Everywhere , the wise find life sweeter than death .

sections.label: Section 1510
sections.original: οὔτι που κραυγὴν ἔθηκας Μενέλεῳ βοηδρομεῖν ;
sections.translation: I suppose that shouting of yours was not for Menelaus to come to the rescue ?

sections.label: Section 1511
sections.original: σοὶ μὲν οὖν ἔγωγʼ ἀμύνειν · ἀξιώτερος γὰρ εἶ .
sections.translation: Oh no ! it was to help you I called out , for you are more deserving .

sections.label: Section 1512
sections.original: ἐνδίκως ἡ Τυνδάρειος ἆρα παῖς διώλετο ;
sections.translation: Did the daughter of Tyndareus die justly , then ?

sections.label: Section 1513
sections.original: ἐνδικώτατʼ , εἴ γε λαιμοὺς εἶχε τριπτύχους θανεῖν .
sections.translation: Most justly , even if she had three throats to die with .

sections.label: Section 1514
sections.original: δειλίᾳ γλώσσῃ χαρίζῃ , τἄνδον οὐχ οὕτω φρονῶν .
sections.translation: Your cowardice makes you glib ; this is not what you really think .

sections.label: Section 1515
sections.original: οὐ γάρ , ἥτις Ἑλλάδʼ αὐτοῖς Φρυξὶ διελυμήνατο ;
sections.translation: Why , surely she deserved it , the one who destroyed Hellas and the Phrygians too ?

sections.label: Section 1516
sections.original: ὄμοσον — εἰ δὲ μή , κτενῶ σε — μὴ λέγειν ἐμὴν χάριν .
sections.translation: Swear you are not saying this to humor me , or I will kill you .

sections.label: Section 1517
sections.original: τὴν ἐμὴν ψυχὴν κατώμοσʼ , ἣν ἂν εὐορκοῖμʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: I swear by my life , an oath I would keep !

sections.label: Section 1518
sections.original: ὧδε κἀν Τροίᾳ σίδηρος πᾶσι Φρυξὶν ἦν φόβος ;
sections.translation: Did every Phrygian in Troy show the same terror of steel as you do ?

sections.label: Section 1519
sections.original: ἄπεχε φάσγανον · πέλας γὰρ δεινὸν ἀνταυγεῖ φόνον .
sections.translation: Take your sword away ! Held so near it flashes a dreadful gleam of blood .

sections.label: Section 1520
sections.original: μὴ πέτρος γένῃ δέδοικας ὥστε Γοργόνʼ εἰσιδών ;
sections.translation: Are you afraid of being turned to a stone , as if you had seen a Gorgon ?

sections.label: Section 1521
sections.original: μὴ μὲν οὖν νεκρός · τὸ Γοργοῦς δʼ οὐ κάτοιδʼ ἐγὼ κάρα .
sections.translation: To a stone , no ! but to a corpse ; I don ’ t know this Gorgon ’ s head .

sections.label: Section 1522
sections.original: δοῦλος ὢν φοβῇ τὸν Ἅιδην , ὅς σʼ ἀπαλλάξει κακῶν ;
sections.translation: A slave , and yet you fear death , which will release you from trouble ?

sections.label: Section 1523
sections.original: πᾶς ἀνήρ , κἂν δοῦλος ᾖ τις , ἥδεται τὸ φῶς ὁρῶν .
sections.translation: Slave or free , every one is glad to gaze upon the light .

sections.label: Section 1524
sections.original: εὖ λέγεις · σῴζει σε σύνεσις . ἀλλὰ βαῖνʼ ἔσω δόμων .
sections.translation: Well said ! Your shrewdness saves you ; go inside .

sections.label: Section 1525
sections.original: οὐκ ἄρα κτενεῖς με ;
sections.translation: You will not kill me after all ?

sections.label: Section 1525b
sections.original: ἀφεῖσαι .
sections.translation: You are spared .

sections.label: Section 1525c
sections.original: καλὸν ἔπος λέγεις τόδε .
sections.translation: How well you said that !
